informal: Let $X$ be a compact Hausdorff space that is the union of the closed subspaces $X_1$ and $X_2$. If $X_1$ and $X_2$ are metrizable, show that $X$ is metrizable.
lean:
theorem Munkres_exercise_34_9
  (X : Type*) [TopologicalSpace X] [CompactSpace X] [T2Space X]
  (X1 X2 : Set X) (hX1 : IsClosed X1) (hX2 : IsClosed X2)
  (hX : X1 ∪ X2 = univ) (hX1m : MetrizableSpace X1)
  (hX2m : MetrizableSpace X2) : MetrizableSpace X
reasoning: The Lean signature captures exactly the informal hypotheses: X is a topological space with CompactSpace and T2Space (compact Hausdorff), X1 and X2 are closed subsets whose union is the whole space (X1 ∪ X2 = univ), and each carries a MetrizableSpace instance on the subtype (i.e., they are metrizable as subspaces). The conclusion MetrizableSpace X matches "X is metrizable." The quantifier structure (universal over X) and types align with the informal statement, so this is a faithful encoding.
